methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $190M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 25.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Positron AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Kneron is a Series B semiconductor company founded by Albert Liu and Frank Chang, having raised $190M to date. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of 8 private and 3 public comparables in the Chips & Semiconductors sector, including Stage A/B/C companies such as Etched, d-Matrix, Positron AI, and public benchmarks (NVDA, AMD, ALAB).

Two valuation methods apply to this analysis. Stage-progression modeling, using the $190M total raised as a proxy base and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B progression, implies a valuation of $361M. Peer-median anchoring across 6 same-sector comparables at similar/adjacent stages yields a median valuation of $1.0B, with a range spanning $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples cannot be applied; the semiconductor public universe trades at a 25.3x median multiple, but Kneron's revenue is not disclosed, precluding this cross-check.

The triangulated analysis across applicable methods supports a valuation range of $307M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $680M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around two factors: (1) whether stage-progression or peer-median better captures Kneron's position given its technical maturity relative to listed peers, and (2) the absence of revenue data, which would anchor absolute value more precisely. The peer set shows significant variation by stage and funding history, suggesting caution in weighting any single comparable equally.
